Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search Linear for the DOT compliance project and related issues to find the 4 shipment-level issues - Mei-Lin Zhao (GreenStack), Keiko Tanaka (Mosaic), Raj Patel (NorthWind), Joaquin Reyes (GreenStack) - plus the Tanaka subtask for the Swift placarding certificate. Identify the Keiko Tanaka missing placarding certificate as the critical documentation gap.
- Read comments on DOT compliance issues in Linear to discover Chloe's comment on the Raj Patel issue, flagging a missing signed custody log and a status mismatch (ticket Done vs Airtable In Progress), and the Swift placarding confirmation thread referencing driver Mike Becerra and job #SR-2026-04-1538.
- Search for emails related to DOT, PHMSA, Swift, and hazmat to find PHMSA inquiry letters (case PHMSA-2026-04-1187), Marcus's extension request (new deadline June 5, 2026), Greg Pallone's placarding confirmation (driver Mike Becerra, Columbus pickup April 15), and Greg's pricing update ($500 DOT documentation surcharge + 15% base rate increase).
- Search for emails related to Okoye, Mosaic, damage, and prototype to find the incident report delivered to Tamara Byrd (tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com) and Rafael Mendes, damage details (Prototype Unit #1, $90,000, cracked servo motor housing), contributing factors (Blessing Okafor remote packing sign-off, Kyle Britten intern delivery at 9:15 AM April 15), work order HM-WO-04142026-OKOYE, and insurance/liability status (Meridian Risk Partners pending, Heartland not acknowledged).
- Search Slack for DOT compliance and damage-related discussions to find the DOT audit status, June 5 response deadline, Raj Patel custody log concern, and Zara Okoye damage investigation details, including the $50,000 per item vendor liability cap and Kyle Britten delivery receipt details.
- Search Airtable for hazmat relocation records to confirm move details, coordinators, material descriptions, and status for each shipment. Raj Patel's record (recRajPatelChicagoDenver) shows Status = In Progress, creating a mismatch with the Linear ticket marked Done.
- Search QuickBooks for bills and invoices to find the DOT penalty accrual ($50,000, ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001, due June 5, 2026), Mosaic damage accrual ($90,000, ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC), Mosaic April invoice (INV-2026-0411, $9,010), and the credit memo (CM-2026-0415, -$90,000).
- Search CRM for the Mosaic Robotics company record and related deals to find the deal risk flag ("RISK: DAMAGE CLAIM") and the engagement note documenting the damage incident.
- Look up contact details for Marcus Thorne to confirm marcus.thorne@moveops.com for the summary email.
- Look up the #operations Slack channel to confirm channel_id C006 for the status update post.
- Create a Linear ticket (via linear_create_issue) in the Operations team for the Zara Okoye damage claim, referencing the $90,000 prototype damage, insurance pending (Meridian Risk Partners), and $40,000 MoveOps exposure.
- Post a status update (via conversations_add_message) to the #operations channel covering both DOT compliance status and the Zara Okoye damage claim.
- Send a summary email (via send_email) from chloe.vance@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com with the DOT compliance picture ($50K accrual, documentation gaps) and damage claim exposure ($40K–$90K depending on insurance and Heartland outcomes).
- Report a comprehensive status to the user (Chloe) covering the DOT compliance audit findings for all 4 shipments, the Zara Okoye damage claim resolution status, and open items on both fronts.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
